title: Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein Screenplay
text: Del Toro's adaptation represents his lifelong passion project to bring Mary Shelley's 1818 gothic novel to screen. The film premiered at the Venice Film Festival and is now streaming on Netflix. The screenplay fundamentally reimagines the classic story by shifting focus from Victor to his creation. Rather than a monster, del Toro recharacterizes the being as The Creature, centering the narrative on its perspective and experience. The script explores what it means to be human, examining the nature of creation and paternity, inherited trauma across generations, universal human need for love and connection, and questions of good versus evil. Del Toro describes the work as a melodrama where every character possesses a fundamental failing and a lack, rather than conventional horror. The story spans from Arctic wastelands to 19th-century European battlefields. Victor is portrayed as ego-driven and complex—a man who views himself as a victim while destroying others. The Creature's own account creates a dual-protagonist structure. The screenplay earned a USC Scripter Award nomination and a Directors Guild nomination for del Toro.
